Activate DNS-01 challenge verification. Mandatory for wildcard certificates. Normal domains only use DNS-01 if HTTP-01 is disabled above.
Activate HTTP-01 challenge verification. For normal domains (no wildcards), this takes priority over DNS-01. Wildcards always use DNS-01. No automatic fallback on failure.
Allow ACME to stop conflicting adapters: Allow ACME to stop conflicting adapters on HTTP-01 port
At least one challenge mechanism must be: At least one challenge mechanism must be active
Connections from the Internet to port 80:
Connections from the Internet to port 80 of FQDN must arrive here.
Setup your firewall/port forwarding appropriately.
D must be alphanumeric only. No special: D must be alphanumeric only. No special characters or space. Common name cannot be blank
Enable this only if ACME should temporar: Enable this only if ACME should temporarily stop another adapter on the same HTTP-01 port. Only ioBroker adapters can be stopped (not other services on the host). This works only for adapters using the standard ioBroker port/bind configuration schema.
ID is internal identifier used to refere:
ID is internal identifier used to reference a collection and must be alphanumeric only.
Common and Alt names are passed to CA.
Optional API base URL for custom acme-dn: Optional API base URL for custom acme-dns instances. Leave empty to use https://auth.acme-dns.io
Optional delegation alias for providers: Optional delegation alias for providers where the adapter writes TXT records directly (for example deSEC or DuckDNS). Enter only the domain, without _acme-challenge.
Leave empty for automatic registration o: Leave empty for automatic registration on the default server. For custom servers, add a row with collection ID and base URL.
